finding: The core peer execution runtime must accept recorded-indeterminate-reconciled as an appendable terminal journal receipt and preserve that exact state as evidence rather than normalizing it in a filesystem adapter.
design_implication: Reconciliation semantics now live at the runtime boundary shared by all terminal journal implementations. A reconciled post-rename write appends the terminal event once, while already-recorded remains non-appendable.

verification.source: exact committed source returned by GitHub contents API
verification.test: deterministic Node test committed for normal record, duplicate suppression, injected directory-sync failure, exact state preservation, and one append
verification.runtime: not executed in this connector-only run

uncertainty: The committed test has not yet produced a GitHub Actions result for these commits.
falsification_route: Fail if reconciled state is rejected or rewritten.
Fail if the terminal event is not appended exactly once.
Fail if an already-recorded receipt causes another append.
privacy_boundary: No private chat, health, credential, or identifying material is included.
